title: ScottyLabs MCP server manifest
description: The registry contract. The MCP gateway reads each server's manifest.yaml to register it, enforce per-tool scope and impact, gate impact:high behind human approval, and decide which committees may use it (design Section 7.4).

owner.description: Committee + maintainer responsible for this server, e.g. 'events-committee'.

description.description: One- or two-sentence summary of what the server does.

endpoint.description: Where the gateway reaches the server. Path A: http://<name>.railway.internal:8080/mcp (private network). Path B: the server's public Railway URL. Must end in /mcp.

transport.description: MCP transport. Every ScottyLabs server speaks Streamable HTTP at a single /mcp endpoint (Section 7.6).

auth.description: How the gateway authenticates to the server. 'bearer' means the gateway presents MCP_AUTH_TOKEN.

tools.description: Every tool the server exposes. A tool with no declared scope is rejected (fail closed, Section 10.3).

allowed_committees.description: Committees whose roles may use this server. The gateway intersects this with per-tool scope.

lifecycle.description: Registry state. New servers land in 'proposed' and do nothing live until a maintainer promotes them to 'approved' (Section 7.4, 10.3).

name.description: Tool name as exposed over MCP, e.g. 'evaluate_reimbursement'.

scope.description: Capability scope the caller's role must hold, e.g. 'finance.read'. Required — a tool with no scope is rejected.

impact.description: read = no external side effect; write = reversible side effect; high = irreversible (send, delete, book, money) and gated behind human approval at the gateway.
